hero.question: Lightning vs. Flames
hero.prob_take: The market is pricing Lightning at a coin flip plus a slight edge, which means you're paying 60.5 cents for a team that's been bleeding value all day and probably doesn't deserve the premium. The 34-point collapse in 24 hours screams sharp money exiting, so unless there's a fresh injury report flip or lineup news, this looks like a fade setup where the crowd got too high on Tampa.

daily_take.headline: Lightning collapsed 34 points in 24 hours. Here's why the market overcorrected.
daily_take.deck: Calgary just torched Edmonton 7-2, and bettors panicked into selling Lightning at 60.5% like it was a bankruptcy filing. The move is dramatic but backwards. A team doesn't lose 35 points of win probability because one of their opponents had a good night. If you're short Lightning right now, you're fighting the Oilers narrative instead of playing the actual matchup. Contrarian signal: buy the dip on any news-driven 34-point drop in a 2-team market.
